game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **The House of Da Vinci** Players explore a 3D Renaissance workshop in first-person, using swipe gestures to look around and pinch-to-zoom for closer inspection of intricate mechanical puzzles. The core gameplay involves interacting with detailed 3D objects via tap-and-drag or rotate gestures to manipulate gears, levers, and hidden compartments, solving complex puzzles inspired by Leonardo Da Vinci's inventions. A unique "Oculus Perpetua" gauntlet, activated by a dedicated UI button, allows players to see through surfaces to reveal hidden mechanisms or rewind specific environmental events to uncover clues. Progression is linear, with each room functioning as an escape puzzle where solving 3-5 interconnected mechanical challenges unlocks access to the next area and advances the overarching narrative of the master's disappearance. The game concludes upon solving the final grand puzzle and uncovering the truth, with no explicit loss conditions beyond failing to solve a puzzle.
game_info.description: Explore a mysterious Renaissance workshop filled with intricate mechanical puzzles inspired by Leonardo Da Vinci's inventions. Each room contains interconnected puzzle elements—gears, levers, and hidden compartments—that you must manipulate to unlock the next area. Use the special Oculus Perpetua device to reveal hidden mechanisms and solve the mystery of the master's disappearance. Navigate through multiple rooms, solving increasingly complex puzzles to reach the final revelation.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Look around the workshop (rotate camera view)
Z: Interact with puzzle elements in front of you (activate/manipulate)
Space: Toggle Oculus Perpetua vision (reveals hidden mechanisms)
Enter: Start game from title screen
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart game (return to title screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic movement and interaction mechanics. Validates that the player can look around, interact with puzzle elements, and that these interactions register correctly in the game state.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Systematically rotate camera in all directions, then attempt to interact with visible puzzle elements. Move through accessible areas and test collision detection with walls and objects.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player position and camera angle updates are smooth and responsive. Interactions with puzzle elements trigger appropriate state changes. No clipping through walls or objects occurs.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests the complete win condition by solving all puzzles optimally. This validates the full game progression from start to finish, ensuring all puzzle mechanics work correctly and the win state is achievable.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Navigate to each puzzle element in the correct sequence, using optimal interaction patterns. Activate Oculus Perpetua when needed to reveal hidden mechanisms. Solve each room's puzzles in the most efficient order to unlock progression.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: All puzzles are solved successfully, all rooms are unlocked in sequence, and the game reaches GAME_OVER_WIN state. The final puzzle solution triggers the ending narrative and completion screen.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests the Oculus Perpetua vision system and its integration with puzzle mechanics. Validates that hidden mechanisms are properly revealed and that the vision toggle works correctly.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Toggle Oculus vision on and off repeatedly while exploring. Approach puzzle elements that require vision to solve and verify that hidden components become visible and interactive.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Oculus vision toggles smoothly without errors. Hidden mechanisms become visible when active. Puzzles requiring vision can only be solved when the Oculus is activated.
TEST_3.description: Tests the Oculus Perpetua vision system and its integration with puzzle mechanics. Validates that hidden mechanisms are properly revealed and that the vision toggle works correctly.

TEST_3.expected: Oculus vision toggles smoothly without errors. Hidden mechanisms become visible when active. Puzzles requiring vision can only be solved when the Oculus is activated.

TEST_4.test_description: Tests edge cases and boundary conditions including attempting to interact with objects out of range, rapid input sequences, and state transitions during puzzle solving.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Attempt interactions from maximum range, spam interaction keys, try to leave rooms before puzzles are solved, and test pausing during critical puzzle moments.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Game handles all edge cases gracefully without crashes or state corruption. Out-of-range interactions fail appropriately. Rapid inputs don't break puzzle state. Pause works correctly at all times.

TEST_4.expected: Game handles all edge cases gracefully without crashes or state corruption. Out-of-range interactions fail appropriately. Rapid inputs don't break puzzle state. Pause works correctly at all times.
